newProperties.name: cloud_storage_gc_max_segments_per_run
newProperties.description: Maximum number of segments to delete per housekeeping run. Each segment maps to up to three object keys (data, index, tx manifest), so a value of 300 produces 600 to 900 deletes plus one per spillover manifest.

newProperties.name: cloud_topics_disable_level_zero_gc_for_tests
newProperties.description: Disables the level-zero garbage collector in cloud topics. This property exists to simplify testing and shouldn't be set in production.

newProperties.name: cloud_topics_disable_metastore_flush_loop_for_tests
newProperties.description: Disables the metastore flush loop in cloud topics. The property exists to simplify testing of read replicas and shouldn't be set in production.

newProperties.name: cloud_topics_epoch_service_max_same_epoch_duration
newProperties.description: The duration of time that a node can use the exact same epoch.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_storage_prefetch_segments_max
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Maximum number of small segments (size <= chunk size) to prefetch ahead during sequential reads. Set to 0 to disable cross-segment prefetch.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_allow_materialization_failure
changedDescriptions.newDescription: When enabled, the reconciler tolerates missing L0 extent objects (404 errors) during materialization. Failed extents are skipped, producing L1 state with empty offset ranges where deleted data was. Use this to recover partitions after accidental deletion of live extent objects.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_compaction_interval_ms
changedDescriptions.newDescription: How often to trigger background compaction for cloud topics.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_compaction_key_map_memory
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Maximum number of bytes that may be used on each shard by cloud topics compaction key-offset maps.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_compaction_max_object_size
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Maximum size in bytes for L1 objects produced by cloud topics compaction.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_fetch_debounce_enabled
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Enables fetch debouncing in cloud topics. This mechanism guarantees that the broker fetches every object only once improving the performance and lowering the cost.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_gc_health_check_interval
changedDescriptions.newDescription: The interval at which the L0 garbage collector checks cluster health. GC will not proceed while the cluster is unhealthy.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_l1_indexing_interval
changedDescriptions.newDescription: The byte interval at which index entries are created within long term storage objects for cloud topics. Index entries are stored in the object metadata and enable efficient seeking by offset or timestamp within a partition. Lower values produce more index entries (better seek granularity) at the cost of a larger footer.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_long_term_file_deletion_delay
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Delay before deleting stale long term files, allowing concurrent readers (e.g. read replica topics) to finish reading them before removal.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_long_term_flush_interval
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Time interval at which long term storage metadata is flushed to object storage.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_metastore_lsm_apply_timeout_ms
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Timeout for applying a replicated write batch to the local LSM database. This may take longer than usual when L0 compaction is behind and writes are being throttled.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_metastore_replication_timeout_ms
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Timeout for L1 metastore Raft replication and waiting for the STM to apply the replicated write batch.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_num_metastore_partitions
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Number of partitions for the cloud topics metastore topic, used to spread metastore load across the cluster. Higher values allow more parallel metadata operations but reduce the amount of work each partition can batch together. Only takes effect when the metastore topic is first created.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_parallel_fetch_enabled
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Enable parallel fetching in cloud topics. This mechanism improves the throughput by allowing the broker to download data needed by the fetch request using multiple shards.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_preregistered_object_ttl
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Time-to-live for pre-registered L1 objects before they are expired.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_produce_no_pid_concurrency
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Maximum number of concurrent raft replication requests for producers without a producer ID (idempotency disabled). Limits how many no-PID writes can proceed past the producer queue into raft simultaneously.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_produce_write_inflight_limit
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Maximum number of in-flight write requests per shard in the cloud topics write pipeline. Requests that exceed this limit are queued until a slot becomes available.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_reconciliation_interval
changedDescriptions.oldDescription: Time interval after which data is moved from short term storage to long term storage.
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Configuration property: cloud_topics_reconciliation_interval

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_reconciliation_max_interval
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Maximum reconciliation interval for adaptive scheduling.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_reconciliation_max_object_size
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Maximum size in bytes for L1 objects produced by the reconciler. With the default target fill ratio of 0.8, this gives an effective target object size of 64 MiB.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_reconciliation_min_interval
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Minimum reconciliation interval for adaptive scheduling. The reconciler will not run more frequently than this.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_reconciliation_parallelism
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Maximum number, per shard, of concurrent objects built by reconciliation

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_reconciliation_slowdown_blend
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Blend factor for slowing down reconciliation (0.0 to 1.0). Higher values mean reconciliation lowers its frequency faster when trying to find a frequency that produces well-sized objects. Generally this should be lower than the speedup blend, because reconciliation has less opportunities to adapt its frequency when it runs less frequently.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_reconciliation_speedup_blend
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Blend factor for speeding up reconciliation (0.0 to 1.0). Higher values mean reconciliation increases its frequency faster when trying to find a frequency that produces well-sized objects.

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_reconciliation_target_fill_ratio
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Target fill ratio for L1 objects. The reconciler adapts its interval to produce objects at approximately this fill level (0.0 to 1.0).

changedDescriptions.name: cloud_topics_upload_part_size
changedDescriptions.newDescription: The part size in bytes used for multipart uploads. The minimum of 5 MiB is the smallest non-terminal part size allowed by cloud object storage providers.

changedDescriptions.name: delete_topic_enable
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Enable or disable topic deletion via the Kafka DeleteTopics API. When set to false, all topic deletion requests are rejected with error code 73 (TOPIC_DELETION_DISABLED). This is a cluster-wide safety setting that cannot be overridden by superusers. Topics in kafka_nodelete_topics are always protected regardless of this setting.

changedDescriptions.name: internal_rpc_request_timeout_ms
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Default timeout for RPC requests between Redpanda nodes.

changedDescriptions.name: leader_balancer_mode
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Mode of the leader balancer optimization strategy. `calibrated` uses a heuristic that balances leaders based on replica counts per shard. `random` randomly moves leaders to reduce load on heavily-loaded shards. Legacy values `greedy_balanced_shards` and `random_hill_climbing` are treated as `calibrated`.

changedDescriptions.name: log_compaction_max_priority_wait_ms
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Maximum time a priority partition (for example, __consumer_offsets) can wait for compaction before preempting regular compaction.

changedDescriptions.name: nested_group_behavior
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Behavior for handling nested groups when extracting groups from authentication tokens.  Two options are available - none and suffix.  With none, the group is left alone (e.g. '/group/child/grandchild').  Suffix will extract the final component from the nested group (e.g. '/group' -> 'group' and '/group/child/grandchild' -> 'grandchild').

changedDescriptions.name: oidc_group_claim_path
changedDescriptions.newDescription: JSON path to extract groups from the JWT payload.

changedDescriptions.name: partition_autobalancing_node_autodecommission_timeout_sec
changedDescriptions.newDescription: When a node is unavailable for at least this timeout duration, it triggers Redpanda to decommission the node. This property applies only when `partition_autobalancing_mode` is set to `continuous`.

changedDescriptions.name: partition_autobalancing_node_availability_timeout_sec
changedDescriptions.oldDescription: When a node is unavailable for at least this timeout duration, it triggers Redpanda to move partitions off of the node. This property applies only when `partition_autobalancing_mode` is set to `continuous`.
changedDescriptions.newDescription: When a node is unavailable for at least this timeout duration, it triggers Redpanda to move partitions off of the node. This property applies only when `partition_autobalancing_mode` is set to `continuous`.

changedDescriptions.name: redpanda.cloud_topic.enabled
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Configuration property: redpanda.cloud_topic.enabled

changedDescriptions.name: schema_registry_enable_qualified_subjects
changedDescriptions.newDescription: Enable parsing of qualified subject syntax (:.context:subject). When false, subjects are treated literally, as subjects in the default context. When true, qualified syntax is parsed to extract context and subject.

removedDeprecatedProperties.name: alter_topic_cfg_timeout_ms
removedDeprecatedProperties.description: The duration, in milliseconds, that Redpanda waits for the replication of entries in the controller log when executing a request to alter topic configurations. This timeout ensures that configuration changes are replicated across the cluster before the alteration request is considered complete.

removedDeprecatedProperties.name: create_topic_timeout_ms
removedDeprecatedProperties.description: Timeout, in milliseconds, to wait for new topic creation.

removedDeprecatedProperties.name: metadata_status_wait_timeout_ms
removedDeprecatedProperties.description: Maximum time to wait in metadata request for cluster health to be refreshed.

removedDeprecatedProperties.name: node_management_operation_timeout_ms
removedDeprecatedProperties.description: Timeout for executing node management operations.

removedDeprecatedProperties.name: recovery_append_timeout_ms
removedDeprecatedProperties.description: Timeout for append entry requests issued while updating a stale follower.

removedDeprecatedProperties.name: rm_sync_timeout_ms
removedDeprecatedProperties.description: Resource manager's synchronization timeout. Specifies the maximum time for this node to wait for the internal state machine to catch up with all events written by previous leaders before rejecting a request.

removedDeprecatedProperties.name: tm_sync_timeout_ms
removedDeprecatedProperties.description: Transaction manager's synchronization timeout. Maximum time to wait for internal state machine to catch up before rejecting a request.

removedDeprecatedProperties.name: wait_for_leader_timeout_ms
removedDeprecatedProperties.description: Timeout to wait for leadership in metadata cache.
